titleStmt.title: The Minister in Bulgaria ( (Earle) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister in Bulgaria ( Earle ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
402. The Secretary General of the Foreign Office this morning protested orally to the Legation on behalf of his Government against articles he alleges have recently appeared in the American press according to which: (a)
Bulgaria has agreed to take aggressive action against Russia. (b)
In return Germany will give Bulgaria a protectorate over the Crimea and a dominant position in the Black Sea. (c)
There is widespread unrest in Bulgaria and the Government is summarily executing Communists, including Communist deputies.
The Secretary General stated that such articles are worse than anything which has been appearing in the Bulgarian press against the United States (see my 394, September 24).

It was explained to the Secretary General that the American press is not government controlled and consequently the American Government cannot be held responsible for statements made therein. However, I said I would be glad to report his denial to my Government.

In my opinion point (b) seems very unlikely. As for point (a), I do not think Bulgaria has at the moment any aggressive intentions against Russia, while I cannot conscientiously say there is, in point (c), no element of truth.
